Technical Case Studies

Academic Projects

COMMUNICATION TECH

Yagi-Uda Antenna Design

Designed and built an 8-element Yagi-Uda antenna tuned for 694 MHz (Channel 21), using one driven element, one reflector, and six directors for high gain and directivity. Simulated in MMANA-GAL and validated with SWR and radiation-pattern testing.

ALUMINUM FABRICATION MMANA-GAL SIMULATION RF SIGNAL OPTIMIZATION
Instrumentation

Water Tank Detector

Dual op-amp comparator circuit that lights a green LED when the tank is low and triggers a buzzer when full — analog sensing with no microcontroller.

sensors OP-AMP / IC DESIGN
Experimental Analysis

Microwave Communication & Electronics Experiments

Hands-on oscilloscope waveform analysis of MHz-range signals, antenna testing, breadboard RF prototyping, and transistor amplifier biasing on calibrated lab equipment.

waves WAVEGUIDE / LABORATORY
Hardware Prototyping

PCB Assembly Projects

Designed and built a PCB from scratch around the LM358N dual op-amp — laid out, soldered, and tested as a comparator system with diode protection.

developer_board SMD / THT SOLDERING
Power Electronics

LM317 Variable PSU

Built a variable power supply unit around the LM317 regulator, covering PCB layout, soldering, and testing, delivering adjustable output for powering and troubleshooting circuits.

bolt REGULATED SUPPLY
Embedded Systems

Battery Alarm Monitoring with Arduino

Prototype circuit using a temperature sensor and potentiometer to detect battery overheating and trigger LED alerts, built on Arduino with calibrated sensing.

battery_alert ARDUINO / SENSORS
